title: Eyes on arts: Upcoming entertainment highlights
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.

Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts. These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis. Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


> 26 February to 8 March


Memoirs of a Chocolate Factory (19:00 with Saturday matinees 14:00) at George Arts Theatre. A vibrant, witty, and heartwarming musical that reimagines the beloved characters of the classic chocolate factory story - now grown up and facing a very different world. Tickets cost R120 (members R100) and are available through Computicket.


> 6 Maart


Joodse feesmaal vir Fynproewers (09:00) by die Bahia Bleu-restaurant by die Diaz-hotel. Die Suid-Kaap Fynproewersgilde word getrakteer in die wondere van die Joodse kookkuns deur Tracy Klass van die restaurant, A Taste of Klass.

Klass, ook 'n bekende komediant, kom haar liefde vir kos met fynproewers deel. Dit kom al die pad van haar twee Joodse tantes wat in Litaue gewoon het. Die wonderlikste Joodse smake, kleure en geure gaan gaste betower. Die dag beloof om 'n heerlike ondervinding te wees. Bespreek by Ena Jacobs by 082 416 5304.


> 8 March


Fundraiser concert with Voldi, Nikki and Tashia (18:00) at the Market Theatre. Enjoy an evening of music throughout the ages and support a fundraiser for Dawie Hechter who was diagnosed with Non-Hodgkin lymphoma, a type of cancer that develops in the lymphatic system. Hechter needs to start intense treatment in Cape Town.

Tickets cost R100 and are available from Alta (082 325 4989) or Tori (061 238 3318) or Heather (072 369 3304).


> 15 March


Presentation on the making of Obie Oberholzer's new book, Going Dutch (12:00) at George Arts Theatre. Going Dutch is Oberholzer's 16th publication, and covers nine South African towns with Dutch names and their mother cities in the Netherlands. Admission: R100. Contact Paul Rixom on 072 525 0016.

One of the striking photographs in Obie Oberholzer's new book, Going Dutch.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
